timeline.notes: Pre-roll. Walking onto mat.
Greig (black) and Anthony (red) facing each other. Approaching.

timeline.visual_reasoning: CLEAR slap/bump visible. Greig (black, left) and Anthony (red, right) both have arms extended, hands touching in a handshake/slap. Classic BJJ roll start.
timeline.notes: ROLL START — slap/bump. Both players touch hands.
Anthony immediately sits to guard after the slap. Greig standing.
timeline.coaching_tip: Control feet before engaging.

timeline.notes: Greig engaging Anthony's seated guard from standing.
timeline.coaching_tip: Grip knees or ankles before committing weight.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Decision tree: Q1 mirrored? Not yet — Anthony initiating one-sided leg hook.
timeline.notes: Anthony entering leg entanglement. Hooking Greig's leg.
timeline.coaching_tip: Backstep when you feel the hook.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Decision tree: Q1 mirrored? YES. Q2 same direction? YES. Q3 outside leg? NO → fifty_fifty.
timeline.notes: 50/50 established. Symmetrical leg entanglement.
timeline.coaching_tip: Fight for top position. Control the far knee.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Decision tree: Q1 mirrored? YES. Q3 outside leg over hip? YES → eighty_twenty for Greig.
timeline.notes: Greig transitions from 50/50 to 80/20. Outside leg gaining dominance.
timeline.coaching_tip: Use 80/20 to backstep out into passing.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Decision tree: Q1 mirrored? YES. Q3 outside leg? NO → fifty_fifty again.
timeline.notes: Second 50/50 entry. Anthony re-engages.
timeline.coaching_tip: Anthony keeps pulling you in. Control his feet at the entry.

timeline.notes: Greig gaining top control. Escaping the entanglement.
timeline.coaching_tip: Backstep to clear and pass.

timeline.notes: Greig extracting from leg entanglement. Mid-pass.
timeline.coaching_tip: Speed — pass before Anthony reguards.

timeline.notes: Greig in top half guard. One leg still trapped.
timeline.coaching_tip: Crossface and knee slide.

timeline.notes: Pass complete! Greig in side control.
timeline.coaching_tip: Advance to mount within 5 seconds.

timeline.notes: Side control maintained.
timeline.coaching_tip: Look for americana or advance to mount.

timeline.notes: Anthony recovers to half guard. First reguard.
timeline.coaching_tip: Need faster advancement from side control.

timeline.notes: Open guard. Anthony has recovered fully.
timeline.coaching_tip: Try a different pass approach.

timeline.notes: Anthony playing butterfly guard.
timeline.coaching_tip: Stay low, control knees, deny underhook.

timeline.notes: Greig in headquarters. Setting up pass.
timeline.coaching_tip: Commit to knee slide with crossface.

timeline.notes: Half guard battle. Greig applying crossface.
timeline.coaching_tip: Win the underhook battle.

timeline.notes: Second guard pass. Side control.
timeline.coaching_tip: Go directly to mount this time.

timeline.notes: Side control maintained.
timeline.coaching_tip: Advance NOW.

timeline.notes: Anthony recovers to half guard again.
timeline.coaching_tip: Same pattern. Must advance faster.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Decision tree: Q1 mirrored? YES → fifty_fifty. Third entry.
timeline.notes: Third 50/50 entry! Anthony's go-to move.
timeline.coaching_tip: Address this at the entry point.

timeline.notes: Greig passes again from 50/50.
timeline.coaching_tip: ADVANCE immediately.

timeline.notes: Greig in headquarters.
timeline.coaching_tip: Commit to the pass.

timeline.notes: Open guard exchange.
timeline.coaching_tip: Mix up passing.

timeline.notes: Greig on top with VERY heavy pressure. Working a submission from side control. Anthony being smothered.
timeline.coaching_tip: Great pressure. Keep the crossface tight and look to isolate the arm.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Anthony taps. Greig on top in tight side control with submission pressure. Anthony concedes.
timeline.notes: TAP! Anthony taps to Greig's pressure/submission from side control. ROLL ENDS.
timeline.coaching_tip: Great finish. The pressure passing paid off — the side control was so heavy Anthony had to tap.

summary.session_overview: Roll 1 at Tempo Jiu Jitsu. 2:23 roll (0:03 slap/bump to 2:26 tap). Greig (black) navigated two 50/50 leg entanglements using 80/20 escapes, passed Anthony's (red) guard multiple times from side control, and secured a SUBMISSION at 2:26 from heavy side control pressure. A dominant performance on top with clean passing and relentless pressure.

key_moments.description: Anthony hooks Greig's leg → entering 50/50 from seated guard.
key_moments.suggestion: Control feet before engaging seated guard.

key_moments.description: Greig transitions from 50/50 to 80/20 using outside leg dominance.
key_moments.suggestion: This is the correct 50/50 escape. Backstep out faster next time.

key_moments.description: First guard pass to side control.
key_moments.suggestion: Good pass. Next time advance to mount immediately.
You know it's coming. Prevent mirrored leg wrapping.

key_moments.description: Greig achieves heavy side control with submission pressure.
key_moments.suggestion: Great pressure. The relentless passing paid off.

key_moments.description: TAP! Anthony taps to Greig's pressure/submission from side control.
key_moments.suggestion: Dominant finish. The top pressure game worked perfectly.

top_3_improvements: 50/50 defence: Got pulled in twice. Control Anthony's feet before engaging his seated guard.
Passing consolidation: Passed 3+ times but kept losing side control. Advance to mount within 3 seconds of each pass.
Faster 80/20 escape: You found the right technique but took time to extract. Drill the backstep.
strengths_observed: Relentless top pressure that led to a submission — the passing paid off
80/20 escape from 50/50 — correct Danaher system technique
Multiple clean guard passes from headquarters and knee slide
summary.guard_retention_reason: N/A — Greig was on top for the entire roll. Never needed bottom guard retention.
summary.positional_awareness_reason: Good awareness of 50/50 escapes and passing opportunities. Lost points for getting caught in 50/50 three times.
summary.transition_quality_reason: Clean passes, smooth 50/50 escapes, and finished with a submission from side control. Would be higher with faster mount advancement.
summary.overall_notes: A WINNING roll. Greig dominated on top throughout, navigated the 50/50 exchanges well, and finished with a submission. The key areas to improve are preventing the 50/50 entries and advancing from side control to mount faster — but the outcome was a clear win.
